title: Agent Workflow Lab
summary: Hands-on workshop for composing agent steps, retrieval context, and demo-ready workflow loops.
detail: This workshop should be the first stop because it directly converts Maya's frontend/product skills into an agentic workflow implementation path.
nextSteps: Capture reusable workflow patterns for the demo

title: Pair with Omar, AI infra engineer
summary: AI infrastructure teammate with retrieval, evals, and deployment experience.
detail: Omar complements Maya's product/frontend strengths with the retrieval and deployment skills needed to ship a credible agent demo.

title: Mentor: Nina, Developer Tools PM
summary: Developer tools product mentor who can help scope the MVP and Demo Day story.
detail: Nina is useful after the first prototype exists because she can sharpen the MVP boundary and judge-facing story.
nextSteps: Ask for one cut and one demo improvement

title: Sponsor credits: Vector DB starter pack
summary: Sponsor resource that speeds up retrieval setup for an agentic workflow demo.
detail: The starter pack removes infrastructure friction so the team can focus on recommendation quality and Demo Day polish.
nextSteps: Connect retrieval output to the recommendation engine

title: On-chain Credentials Clinic
summary: Workshop for issuing simple achievement or skill credentials after builder milestones.
detail: This is a later-stage workshop for turning BuildMate ASG's skill graph into portable proof of builder progress.

title: Meet Priya, smart contract builder
summary: Smart contract teammate who can help turn skill achievements into verifiable credentials.
detail: Priya is a good stretch teammate if the team decides to add on-chain skill credentials to the demo scope.
nextSteps: Decide if on-chain proof belongs in MVP
